title: Pilgrim's Progress §183: CHR.
body: CHR. I am glad, my brother, that thou didst withstand this villain so bravely; for of all, as thou sayest, I think he has the wrong name; for he is so bold as to follow us in the streets, and to attempt to put us to shame before all men: that is, to make us ashamed of that which is good; but if he was not himself audacious, he would never attempt to do as he does. But let us still resist him; for notwithstanding all his bravadoes, he promoteth the fool and none else. "The wise shall inherit glory, said Solomon, but shame shall be the promotion of fools." FAITH. I think we must cry to Him for help against Shame, who would have us to be valiant for the truth upon the earth. CHR. You say true; but did you meet nobody else in that valley? FAITH. No, not I; for I had sunshine all the rest of the way through that, and also through the Valley of the Shadow of Death.
author: engine

witnesses.label: First edition Nathaniel Ponder, London 1678 — original imprint
Cited extensively by Spurgeon, Edwards, modern Reformed writers
